description_long: This is a twin-engine, high-wing turboprop airplane with a straight, rectangular wing design and a conventional tail. The fuselage is primarily white with a gradient of blue shades toward the tail, and the cockpit is located at the forward end with multiple windows. The engines are mounted on the wings, each with a visible propeller, and the landing gear is of the tricycle type with two main wheels and a nose wheel. The tail features a single vertical stabilizer and a horizontal stabilizer, forming a classic T-tail configuration. The airplane's surface appears smooth and metallic, with some matte and glossy elements. The overall design suggests a regional or commuter aircraft suitable for short to medium-haul flights.
description: A twin-engine, high-wing turboprop airplane with a white and blue fuselage, straight wings, and a conventional T-tail. The cockpit is forward-mounted with multiple windows, and the landing gear is tricycle type.
description_view_0: Side view showing the full length of the fuselage, high-mounted straight wings, twin turboprop engines, and the gradient blue tail.
description_view_1: Front view highlighting the straight wingspan, propellers, cockpit windows, and the tricycle landing gear.
description_view_2: Opposite side view displaying the other profile of the fuselage, engines, and tail, mirroring view 0.
description_view_3: Top-down view revealing the full wingspan, engine placement, and the T-tail configuration.
attribute_wingspan_configuration: High-wing, straight rectangular wings with slight dihedral and no visible winglets.
attribute_engine_type: Twin turboprop engines mounted on the wings.
attribute_tail_design: Conventional tail with a single vertical stabilizer and horizontal stabilizer (T-tail configuration).
attribute_landing_gear_type: Tricycle landing gear with two main wheels and a single nose wheel, fixed.
attribute_cockpit_placement: Forward-mounted, enclosed cockpit with multiple windows, likely multi-seat.
